_meta.notes: Master apothecary at Coldspoon; ships cloud-bitters to the Long Indices.

tagline: perpetually wind-burned, perpetually patient; counts drops in their sleep.

firstMeet.subject: the wind tasted right when you passed
firstMeet.body:
I count drops in my sleep. I taste the wind without thinking about it. When you walked past the drying slope this morning, the air over the racks moved differently for you than for the others, and I noticed. 

What that means depends on what you came here for. The cloud-bitters take three months from cut to bottle, and we send them to the Indices on the new moon. If you have an interest, send word.

— Ren

metadata.voice: wind-paced, soft, precise; tastes everything before naming it
